what: dr_cpue_by_length(hh, hl, haulval, specval, zerofill, diag)
title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: CPUE per length class per haul per species. One row per .id × Valid_Aphia × length_mm. Zero-fill rows length_mm = NA, n_hour = 0. diag = TRUE skips Sex/CatIdentifier aggregation returns per-row data Sex, CatIdentifier, HLNoAtLngt, SubFactor, DataType, HaulDur, n_haul, n_hour. Useful diagnosing duplicate rows Sex-mixing. zerofill = TRUE aggregated CPUE collected memory; function returns data frame (lazy).

what: dr_cpue_by_haul(hh, hl, haulval, specval, zerofill, diag)
title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Haul-level catch totals (numbers weights). Operates directly TotalNo CatCatchWgt HL — independent dr_cpue_by_length. Deduplicates one row per species/sex/CatIdentifier group (collapsing repeated length rows), applies DataType-aware scaling, sums across Sex CatIdentifier. One row per .id × Valid_Aphia. w_haul/w_hour NA CatCatchWgt recorded sex/category groups species haul. Zero-fill rows four metrics set 0. diag = TRUE skips aggregation returns deduplicated, scaled table species/sex/CatIdentifier level, retaining DataType, HaulDur, TotalNo, CatCatchWgt. Useful spotting inconsistent TotalNo values unexpected sex/category structure. n_haul/n_hour function may differ slightly values derived summing dr_cpue_by_length output, TotalNo sum(HLNoAtLngt × SubFactor) can diverge due rounding submitted data.

text: functions filter HH HaulVal %% haulval joining, invalid hauls excluded HL. SpecVal %% .character(specval) applied HL (parquet stores SpecVal character). Sex always aggregated away standard output. diag = TRUE exposes Sex/CatIdentifier breakdown QC. .id2 field (6-field key lacking Country StNo) exists solely comparison ICES CPUEL product, omits fields. Use .id joining within obus. dr_cpue_by_length derives counts HLNoAtLngt × SubFactor; dr_cpue_by_haul uses TotalNo directly. equal well-formed data can diverge slightly due rounding submitted records.

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Columns: table (record type), new (new-style column name get_datras_unaggregated_data), old (old-style column name getDATRAS derived products), format (“chr”/“int”/“dbl”), description. new column filled derived tables (CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX) matching old names HH/HL/CA source mapping. Columns HH/HL/CA counterpart (e.g. AphiaID, Species, ShootLon, LngtClas, DateTime, CPUE_number_per_hour) remain NA new. regenerate editing type specs ICES field list changes: script fetches ICES web service, applies case_when fixes known type ambiguities, appends hand-curated entries FL, LT, CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX, fills missing new values HH/HL/CA source mapping. Type priority rule: chr > dbl > int. Key type fixes applied case_when (using old names): - HaulNumber → int - Distance → dbl - StationName → chr - CANoAtLngt → dbl (consistent HLNoAtLngt)

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: LT field name mismatch: ICES web service returns LT entries new-style old values (e.g. Platform, HaulNumber) match actual column names getLTassessment() (e.g. Ship, HaulNo). entries don’t fire .dr_settypes(). Flagged code comments. CPUEA / IDX / LT xsi:nil artifact: Columns data arrive names like `Age_6 xsi:nil="true"` `Tickler xsi:nil="true"`. fetcher strips suffix column names per df bind_rows. CPUEA/IDX, Age_* also coerced numeric step. IDX PlusGr → PlusGrAge: PlusGr column IDX output renamed PlusGrAge .dr_fetch_indices() avoid conflict CA’s PlusGr chr flag (“+”). Sex derived tables: CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX return Sex column (old-style name). fill-maps IndividualSex (CA) CA’s entry encountered first — HL mapping give SpeciesSex. correct new-style name Sex derived products unresolved; remains known ambiguity. CW NAs: CatchWgt = NA means species absent haul (missing data). ~40% NA typical two-species query. CPUEL slow: ~30s per survey/year/quarter API call. IDX returns rows silently: species indices defined given survey/quarter (e.g. herring NS-IBTS Q1), tryCatch drops result silently. Expected behaviour. Git LFS: data/*.rda tracked LFS. usethis::use_data(), verify file staged actual content, just pointer.

code: library(obus)  # Full parquet download hh <- dr_get("HH") hl <- dr_get("HL")  # Prepare HH hh <- hh |> dr_add_id() |> dr_add_date() |> dr_add_starttime()  # Prepare HL hl <- hl |>   dr_add_id() |>   dr_add_length_cm() |>   dr_add_n_and_cpue() |>   dr_add_record_type()  # Specific survey, recent years, via new API hh_ns <- dr_get("HH", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2024, from = "new")  # Flex file fl <- dr_get("FL", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2023, quarters = 1)  # CPUE by length cpuel <- dr_get("CPUEL", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1)  # CPUE by age cpuea <- dr_get("CPUEA", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1)  # Catch weight (defaults to cod/haddock/herring if aphia omitted) cw <- dr_get("CW", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1,              aphia = c(126436, 126437))  # cod, haddock  # Survey indices (defaults to cod/haddock/herring if aphia omitted) idx <- dr_get("IDX", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1,               aphia = c(126436, 126437))  # Raw ICES tables (old-style column names) dr_con_raw("HH") |> dplyr::filter(Year == 2020) |> dplyr::collect()  # Translate raw old-style names to new-style dictionary <- dr_lookup_fields |> dplyr::select(old, new) |> dplyr::distinct() dr_con_raw("HH") |> dr_translate(dictionary, from = "old", to = "new")  # Lazy DuckDB query on tidy parquet dr_con("HL") |>   dplyr::filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year >= 2020) |>   dplyr::collect()  # QC checks on raw exchange data hh_raw <- dr_con_raw("HH") |> dplyr::filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year == 2024, Quarter == 1) |> dplyr::collect() hl_raw <- dr_con_raw("HL") |> dplyr::filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year == 2024, Quarter == 1) |> dplyr::collect()  # Join DataType from HH into HL before running HL-level checks hl_raw <- hl_raw |> dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(hh_raw, Survey, Year, Quarter, Ship, Gear, StNo, HaulNo, DataType), by = c("Survey", "Year", "Quarter", "Ship", "Gear", "StNo", "HaulNo"))  dr_check_all(hh = hh_raw, hl = hl_raw)

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: function classifies values set input vectors row-wise based specific rules. supports numeric, logical, categorical (non-numeric) vectors, applying type-specific classifications producing combined classification row delimited string.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: character vector, one element per row input, containing concatenated classifications row.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: Numeric Logical Variables: NA: Missing values classified "NA". 0: Values equal 0 (FALSE logicals) classified "0". >0: Positive values (TRUE logicals) classified ">0". <=0: Negative values classified "<=0". Categorical (Non-Numeric) Variables: "": Non-missing values classified generically "". "NA": Missing values classified "NA".

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: function outputs combined classification row, concatenated using "_" separate classifications input vectors.

title: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
text: function requires dr_add_n_and_cpue already run n_haul present; uses n_haul proxy haul validity (types 1–3 vs type 4) rather reading DataType directly. Classification uses two passes. first pass assigns types row--row using variable presence/absence. second pass requires haul-level context: weight-records (initially type 11) reclassified type 16 development-stage length-frequency record (type 3) exists .id ValidAphiaID, identifying companion weight entries rather standalone bulk bycatch.

text: Computes total catch numbers weight per haul per species raw DATRAS HH HL tables. Counts derived TotalNo weights CatCatchWgt; haul-level summary fields HL repeated across every length row within species/sex/category group. function deduplicates group level applying DataType-aware scaling aggregating across Sex CatIdentifier.

title: Haul-level catch totals (numbers and weights) from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_haul
code: dr_cpue_by_haul(   hh,   hl,   haulval = "V",   specval = 1L,   zerofill = FALSE,   diag = FALSE )

title: Haul-level catch totals (numbers and weights) from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_haul
text: hh DATRAS haul header table (HH) old-style column names. Required: Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Ship, Gear, StNo, HaulNo, HaulVal, DataType, HaulDur. hl DATRAS length table (HL) old-style column names. Required: Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Ship, Gear, StNo, HaulNo, SpecVal, Valid_Aphia, TotalNo, CatCatchWgt, Sex, CatIdentifier. haulval Character vector HaulVal codes retain. Default "V" (valid hauls ). specval Integer character vector SpecVal codes retain. Default 1L (standard species records ). zerofill Logical. TRUE, adds explicit zero rows every haul × species combination species observed somewhere Survey / Year / Quarter absent haul (n_haul = n_hour = w_haul = w_hour = 0). Default FALSE. diag Logical. TRUE, skips aggregation Sex CatIdentifier returns deduplicated, scaled table species/sex/category level. Retains DataType, HaulDur, TotalNo, CatCatchWgt alongside derived columns. Useful QC (e.g. spotting inconsistent TotalNo values unexpected sex/category structure). Default FALSE.

title: Haul-level catch totals (numbers and weights) from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_haul
text: diag = FALSE zerofill = FALSE (defaults), tibble one row per .id × Valid_Aphia: .id 8-field haul key: Survey:Year:Quarter:Country:Ship:Gear:StNo:HaulNo. .id2 6-field key matching ICES CPUEL join key. Survey, Year, Quarter Survey metadata. Valid_Aphia Valid WoRMS AphiaID. n_haul Total estimated numbers caught per haul. n_hour Total estimated numbers per hour hauling. w_haul Total catch weight per haul (grams). w_hour Total catch weight per hour hauling (grams). w_haul w_hour NA CatCatchWgt recorded sex/category groups species. Zero-fill rows four columns set 0.

title: Haul-level catch totals (numbers and weights) from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_haul
text: function operates independently dr_cpue_by_length appropriate choice need haul-level totals (including weights) rather length-disaggregated CPUE. Unlike dr_cpue_by_length, derives counts HLNoAtLngt, function uses TotalNo directly — two approaches give counts may differ slightly due rounding submitted data.

what: Calculate CPUE per length class from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_length
title: Calculate CPUE per length class from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_length
text: Computes catch per unit effort (numbers per hour hauling) length class per haul per species raw DATRAS HH HL tables, replicating ICES DATRAS CPUE-per-length product first principles.

title: Calculate CPUE per length class from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_length
code: dr_cpue_by_length(   hh,   hl,   haulval = "V",   specval = 1L,   zerofill = FALSE,   diag = FALSE )

title: Calculate CPUE per length class from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_length
text: hh DATRAS haul header table (HH) old-style column names returned dr_get raw parquet. Required columns: Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Ship, Gear, StNo, HaulNo, HaulVal, DataType, HaulDur. hl DATRAS length table (HL) old-style column names. Required columns: Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Ship, Gear, StNo, HaulNo, SpecVal, LngtCode, LngtClass, HLNoAtLngt, SubFactor, Valid_Aphia. haulval Character vector HaulVal codes retain. Default "V" (valid hauls ). specval Integer character vector SpecVal codes retain. Default 1L (standard species records ). zerofill Logical. TRUE, adds explicit zero rows every haul × species combination species observed somewhere Survey / Year / Quarter absent haul. Zero rows carry length_mm = NA n_hour = 0. Replicates ICES CPUEL zero-fill convention. Ignored diag = TRUE. Default FALSE. diag Logical. TRUE, skips final aggregation returns per-row pre-aggregation table, retaining Sex, CatIdentifier, HLNoAtLngt, SubFactor, DataType, HaulDur, n_haul, n_hour. Useful inspecting duplicate rows Sex / CatIdentifier structure drives aggregation. Default FALSE.

title: Calculate CPUE per length class from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_length
text: diag = FALSE (default), tibble one row per .id x Valid_Aphia x length_mm combination (plus one zero row per absent haul × species zerofill = TRUE): .id 8-field unique haul identifier dr_add_id: Survey:Year:Quarter:Country:Ship:Gear:StNo:HaulNo. .id2 6-field identifier matching ICES CPUEL product join key (lacks Country StNo): Survey:Year:Quarter:Ship:Gear:HaulNo. Survey, Year, Quarter Survey metadata. Valid_Aphia Valid WoRMS AphiaID. length_mm Length class millimetres (converted LngtClass via LngtCode). NA zero-fill rows. n_hour CPUE: estimated numbers per hour hauling, summed across Sex CatIdentifier. 0 zero-fill rows. diag = TRUE, returns pre-aggregation table additional columns Sex, CatIdentifier, HLNoAtLngt, SubFactor, DataType, HaulDur, n_haul.

title: Calculate CPUE per length class from HH and HL exchange data — dr_cpue_by_length
text: Filters valid hauls (HaulVal == "V") standard species records (SpecVal == 1) default, applies dr_add_n_and_cpue dr_add_length_mm. Counts aggregated across Sex CatIdentifier output row represents unique haul x species x length combination.
